reasoning: The Lean statement asserts existence and uniqueness of a topology T' on X that (i) is a topology, (ii) contains each T_i, and (iii) is contained in every topology containing all T_i. Conditions (ii) and (iii) together are exactly the universal property of the "smallest topology containing all T_α" in the informal statement, and ∃! captures the uniqueness claim. The family is correctly modeled as an indexed family T : I → Set (Set X) with the hypothesis that each T i is a topology, matching "family of topologies on X". No extra or missing hypotheses are introduced, so the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
